note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. Negatives are near-misses that share dependency/security vocabulary but belong to a sibling (sec-secrets, sec-ci, sec-iac, sec-scan, sec-gate, sec-fix, sec-compliance) or a plain migration-plan / package-install / debug action.

queries.text: check our dependencies for known vulnerabilities before the release
run an npm audit on package.json and package-lock and tell me what's exploitable
audit our python deps — requirements.txt and poetry.lock — for CVEs and give me upgrade paths
manager asked for a license check on our third-party packages, worried about GPL in a commercial product
which of our libraries are abandoned or unmaintained? some haven't shipped in years
go.mod has a bunch of transitive deps — are any of them vulnerable and reachable?
generate an SBOM and flag any packages with high-risk licenses
full-stack repo, Cargo.toml on the backend and package.json on the frontend — audit both for vulnerable libs
i think one of our npm packages might be a typosquat, can you review the dependency list
pre-compliance review — need a prioritized report of CVEs across our Gemfile and pom.xml with fix versions
scan the whole git history for leaked API keys and tokens we may have committed
our GitHub Actions pull an action from @main and the token is write-all — audit the pipeline
queries.note: CI/CD supply-chain config → sec-ci, not the dependency manifests

queries.text: the Dockerfile base image is stale and runs as root — review the container for security
just install the latest lodash and update the lockfile
queries.note: plain package install, not a vulnerability audit — shell/edit task

queries.text: our build is failing because two packages want conflicting versions of react — help me sort out the version clash
queries.note: dependency resolution / broken build → debug, not a CVE audit

queries.text: we found CVE-2024-1234 in our jwt library — write me a tested patch and a regression test
queries.note: remediating a known finding → sec-fix, not re-scanning deps

queries.text: plan the migration from moment.js to date-fns across the codebase, with steps and rollback
queries.note: swapping a library is a refactor plan → migration-plan

queries.text: quick security check on my staged diff before I commit — did I add anything risky?
queries.note: fast diff-scoped pre-commit gate → sec-gate (even if it flags a new dep)

queries.text: full OWASP Top 10 audit of the whole service before we ship to prod
queries.note: comprehensive whole-codebase audit → sec-scan (it orchestrates deps as one part)

queries.text: map our code against OWASP ASVS L2 and give me the compliance gap matrix
run a full security audit of our dependencies — CVEs, licenses, and abandoned packages across all our manifests
queries.note: says 'full security audit' but scoped to dependencies → must beat sec-scan

queries.text: one of our npm packages looks like a supply-chain risk — a typosquat pulled in transitively, audit the dependency tree
queries.note: 'supply-chain' overlaps sec-ci but the target is the dependency tree/manifests → must beat sec-ci

queries.text: comprehensive OWASP security scan of the whole app before the acquisition due-diligence, dependencies included
queries.note: whole-codebase OWASP audit that orchestrates the dep scan as one part → sec-scan wins

queries.text: we identified a vulnerable transitive dependency — now generate and verify the minimal upgrade patch with tests
queries.note: finding is already known; producing the tested remediation patch → sec-fix wins
